title: PSI/CrUX parser + best-practices + LighthouseMetrics types + committed fixture + timeout bound

files_forbidden: any component, route handler, or message catalog

acceptance_criteria: PSI request additionally appends category 'best-practices' alongside performance/accessibility/seo (single call).
Parses data.loadingExperience (URL-level) and data.originLoadingExperience (origin-level) defensively — every field optional, total absence is the normal case and must not throw.
CLS field percentile (CUMULATIVE_LAYOUT_SHIFT_SCORE.percentile) is normalized by dividing by 100; a unit test asserts raw 10 -> 0.10.
INP (INTERACTION_TO_NEXT_PAINT) parsed when present; when absent it is treated as no-INP-field-signal and FID is NEVER substituted into an INP slot.
The real-user verdict captured is Google's verbatim values only: per-metric `category` and the top-level `overall_category` exactly as PSI returns them (no self-computed Good/NI/Poor bucketing).

categories['best-practices'].score parsed into a new nullable LighthouseMetrics field; a curated set of failed best-practices audits parsed via the existing parseAudit, tolerant of audits missing per Lighthouse version, with NO reliance on the removed 'no-vulnerable-libraries' audit.

LighthouseMetrics interface extended with new OPTIONAL/nullable fields (fieldData, originFieldData, bestPracticesScore, bestPracticesIssues, and per-metric CrUX p75+category); the emptyMetrics / fail-safe object includes every new field as null/[] so all existing fail-soft return paths stay valid.
For the multi-category call PAGESPEED_TIMEOUT_MS is 45000 and MAX_RETRIES is 0; a test asserts PAGESPEED_TIMEOUT_MS * (MAX_RETRIES + 1) <= 50000 (well under SCAN_TIMEOUT_MS=120000).

A real captured PSI v5 JSON response is committed at src/__tests__/fixtures/psi-v5-sample.json (must contain a real loadingExperience block with CLS, INP, LCP and a best-practices category); a test parses it and asserts all new fields populate without error. If a live capture cannot be obtained, use a documented Google-published sample and record the provenance in a top-level _comment field.
All existing lighthouse.ts behaviour (existing return shape consumers, 429/403/timeout fail-safe paths) is preserved; npm run lint and npm run test pass for touched code.
output_format: code plus a plain-English explanation of every non-obvious decision (CLS normalization, INP/FID handling, fixture provenance, timeout math)
